_source: CPIC and PharmGKB CYP1A2 drug guidelines

clozapine.Normal Metabolizer: Use standard recommended dose. Monitor plasma clozapine concentrations. Smoking status significantly alters CYP1A2 activity — dose adjustment required if smoking status changes.
clozapine.Intermediate Metabolizer: Consider 25% dose reduction. Monitor plasma clozapine concentrations closely. Reduce dose if patient is a non-smoker.
clozapine.Poor Metabolizer: Consider 50% dose reduction. High risk of clozapine toxicity (seizures, agranulocytosis). Therapeutic drug monitoring essential.
clozapine.Rapid Metabolizer (when induced): Patients who smoke may require up to 1.5x higher doses. Abrupt smoking cessation can cause 50% increase in clozapine levels — monitor closely.

theophylline.Normal Metabolizer: Initiate with standard dose. Monitor serum theophylline levels. Target 5-15 mcg/mL.
theophylline.Intermediate Metabolizer: Initiate with 75% of standard dose. Monitor levels closely.
theophylline.Poor Metabolizer: Initiate with 50% of standard dose. High risk of toxicity (nausea, arrhythmia, seizures). Frequent monitoring required.
theophylline.Rapid Metabolizer (when induced): Higher doses may be needed. Monitor serum levels to confirm therapeutic range.

caffeine.Poor Metabolizer: Increased sensitivity to caffeine effects. Elevated cardiovascular risk at high doses. Consider reduction.
caffeine.Rapid Metabolizer (when induced): Rapid caffeine clearance. Lower cardiovascular risk associated with high consumption.
